region_of_origin: Netherlands (HEINEKEN International); imported and distributed in Singapore by HEINEKEN Asia Pacific

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI specific to Heineken lager; closest parent 'beer (food product)' used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code specific to Heineken lager; 'Beer (substance)' used as closest parent.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330ml bottle of Heineken Lager, the typical single-serve portion available in Singapore supermarkets, pubs, restaurants, and hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: Heineken Lager — USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) and Heineken Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded) and HEINEKEN Asia Pacific product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Heineken Lager and cross-referenced against the Heineken Singapore product label. Values are per 100ml (treated as per 100g for liquid). The Singapore market 5% ABV formulation is identical in composition to the global Heineken Lager formulation per HEINEKEN Asia Pacific.

gi_source.title: Alcoholic beverages — beer, lager: glycemic index data from University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate for standard lager beer at 5% ABV)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Heineken Lager specifically. GI of 66 (medium) is the closest defensible analogous estimate for standard lager beer (5% ABV) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 330ml serving calculated as: GI (66) × carbohydrate per serving (12.87g) / 100 = 8.5. Alcohol itself does not raise blood glucose but the residual carbohydrate (3.9g per 100ml; 12.87g per 330ml bottle) contributes a moderate glycemic load.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol-mediated blood pressure elevation; each 330ml bottle equals approximately 1.3 standard drinks
hypertension.guidance: Regular consumption of 2 or more 330ml bottles of Heineken per day raises systolic blood pressure by approximately 4 mmHg; patients with hypertension should limit intake to no more than 1 bottle per day and those with uncontrolled hypertension should abstain entirely, as alcohol is a clinically significant and under-counselled contributor to treatment-resistant hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis and masks hypoglycaemia symptoms; drug interactions with sulfonylureas and metformin; 12.87g residual carbohydrate per bottle
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes on sulfonylureas must avoid Heineken or consume it only with food due to the risk of dangerous delayed hypoglycaemia (4–8 hours post-ingestion); patients on metformin face lactic acidosis risk at high alcohol doses; all T2DM patients should never drink on an empty stomach and should ensure an evening snack if alcohol is consumed to prevent nocturnal hypoglycaemia.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol raises triglycerides, contributes to atrial fibrillation risk, and adds empty calories; low sodium and zero saturated fat are neutral factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with established cardiovascular disease should limit Heineken to no more than 1 bottle (1.3 standard drinks) per occasion and avoid regular daily consumption, as chronic alcohol intake raises triglycerides, increases atrial fibrillation risk, and contributes to hypertension, all of which worsen cardiovascular outcomes; the low sodium (5mg per 100ml) and zero saturated fat content are not clinically meaningful benefits in this context.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Hepatotoxic drug interaction with methotrexate (DMARD); alcohol promotes IL-6 elevation counteracting DMARD therapy
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis on methotrexate must avoid all alcohol including Heineken, as even low alcohol doses are hepatotoxic in this context; ACR guidelines recommend complete abstinence; patients not on methotrexate should limit to 1–2 standard drinks per occasion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Heineken Lager is a pale lager beer brewed by HEINEKEN International (Netherlands) and imported into Singapore by HEINEKEN Asia Pacific. The Singapore market 330ml bottle contains 5% ABV and is identical in nutritional composition to the global Heineken Lager formulation. Per 100ml, Heineken provides 43 kcal, 0.3g protein, 0.0g fat, and 3.9g carbohydrate, yielding approximately 142 kcal, 12.87g carbohydrate, and 13.2g alcohol per 330ml bottle. The beer is brewed from water, malted barley, hops, yeast, and maize; it is filtered and pasteurised. Heineken is nutritionally near-identical to Tiger Beer at equivalent volume and ABV. Clinically, the primary concerns are alcohol-mediated: sulfonylurea-associated hypoglycaemia risk (delayed 4–8 hours), metformin lactic acidosis risk at high doses, methotrexate hepatotoxicity in rheumatoid arthritis patients, and blood pressure elevation with regular consumption. The glycemic load per 330ml bottle is approximately 8.5 (medium), driven by residual malt carbohydrate. Heineken is widely available across Singapore supermarkets, pubs, restaurants, and hawker centres. It is vegetarian, vegan, and dairy-free but contains gluten from barley and is not halal. Common Chinese names include 喜力 and 喜力啤酒 (Xǐlì Píjiǔ); the Malay name is Bir Heineken; the Tamil name is ஹைனேகன் பீர்; the Hindi name is हेनेकेन बियर. The taste is crisp, clean, and slightly bitter with a balanced malt sweetness.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) and cross-referenced with the Heineken Singapore product label. The Singapore market 5% ABV formulation is confirmed identical to the global Heineken Lager formulation by HEINEKEN Asia Pacific. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for standard lager beer at 5% ABV, which is the closest defensible approved-source match.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Heineken Lager — beer is a fermented grain beverage with no fat by formulation; confirmed zero. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — residual sugars are fully fermented in this lager formulation; confirmed zero. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — filtered lager contains no dietary fibre; confirmed zero. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to 0.0 as beer contains no fat by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per product label. water_g set to -1: detailed moisture data for Heineken Lager is not separately reported as a nutrient field in the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry; the value is implicit (beer is predominantly water at approximately 92g per 100ml) but not explicitly tabulated in the approved source, therefore -1 is used per numeric hygiene rules. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate for standard lager beer (5% ABV) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct Heineken-specific GI entry exists in any approved source. Glycemic load per 330ml serving = GI (66) × carbohydrate (12.87g) / 100 = 8.5. The input record description was truncated; full clinical context has been reconstructed from approved sources and the complete input data. Aliases for 喜力 and 喜力啤酒 (Xǐlì Píjiǔ) are retained from input; language tag corrected from 'en' to 'chinese' in aliases_by_language. Tamil local name ஹைனேகன் பீர் and Hindi local name हेनेकेन बियर are transliterations of the brand name included to satisfy multilingual alias coverage requirements; these are phonetic renderings of the international brand name in Tamil and Hindi scripts. The foodex2_match_type was corrected from 'direct' to 'exact' for consistency with template.
